subject: [PATCH v7 0/2] mm: improve large folio readahead for exec memory

2026-06-02.patch_summary: This patch improves large folio readahead performance for executable memory by simplifying the logic and capping the maximum THP (Transparent Huge Pages) order to 2M. The changes are based on benchmark results that show a significant improvement in cold fault and random access times, with an average reduction of 50% and 23%, respectively. The patch builds upon previous revisions and includes suggestions from multiple contributors to ensure correctness and performance.

reviews.author: Pedro Falcato
reviews.summary:
Gave Reviewed-by

Reviewer Pedro Falcato expressed frustration with ad-hoc criteria in filemap/readahead and suggested that they should be formally documented, potentially leading to a future patch.
reviews.raw_body:
Just a side comment: I really hate these adhoc criteria all around
filemap/readahead. One day we ought to actually write things down, and
write things in a way that isn't entirely mysterious. I might see if I send
a patch for this down the line...

reviews.author: Pedro Falcato
reviews.summary: The reviewer, Pedro Falcato, expressed a concern that the patch's performance improvement might be short-lived and potentially lead to degradation over time.
reviews.raw_body:
Reviewed-by: Pedro Falcato <pfalcato@suse.de>

Overall LGTM. Lets see if performance doesn't degrade.

/me hides in cover

-- 
Pedro
